repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry is this elementary support-geometry lemma.

published_prior_art.cite: Elementary conditional second-moment bound; it is supporting machinery rather than a research result.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry audits this exact ordered causal pair.

published_prior_art.cite: AudibertTsybakov2007 supplies the Hölder-margin geometry, but not this full-data compatibility audit.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; the catalogue's stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap concerns policy regret, not this value interval.

published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeVanderLaan2016 establishes root-n optimal-value inference, including exceptional-law settings.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry constructs a randomized-sign causal max-functional family adjacent to an exact-tie law.

published_prior_art.cite: AudibertTsybakov2007 gives generic Hölder-margin constructions, not the displayed dominated full-data causal family with fixed value separation.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat result gives an honest-CI expected-length lower bound for an optimal-policy value at an exact-tie law.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004 supplies the testing-to-length mechanism for Gaussian linear functionals; LuedtkeVanderLaan2016, ShiLuSong2020, WuWang2021, XuGuo2026, and WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025 give optimal-value inference but no verified sparse-mixture uniform-honesty lower bound over this causal ordered pair.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry studies a connected union-honest interval for this optimal-value pair.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004 and ArmstrongKolesar2020 provide adjacent honest-adaptation theory, while current optimal-value papers provide pointwise or procedure-specific inference rather than this upper construction.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry gives this tie-versus-near-tie phase question for an optimal-policy value.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified cited paper states the displayed margin-smoothness boundary for this causal sparse-mixture setting.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:frontier-boundary
soundness_flags.one_line: For 3s_1α>d, the defined exponent has ξ>1/2, so the displayed mixture separation δ_n≈n^{-ξ}=o(n^{-1/2}); this construction is then nonbinding relative to the oracle root-n length and cannot itself have an order-n^{-1/2} “sparse-mixture penalty” as the question states.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Develops expected-length adaptation theory for confidence intervals for linear functionals, with two-point lower bounds and ordered/between-class moduli.
Studies optimal-treatment-rule value inference, gives a pathwise-differentiability characterization, and describes root-n confidence intervals even when the parameter is nonregular.

source_verification_receipts.locator: Abstract, pp. 1–2; pp. 6–7, Conditions (A5)–(A6) and Theorem 2; pp. 12–13, Theorem 5
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Develops subagging confidence intervals for the optimal value, uses a near-zero contrast margin condition, proves nominal coverage, and compares expected interval lengths.

source_verification_receipts.version: Biometrics 77 (2021), pp. 465–476, version of record landing page
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Proposes resampling-based model-free robust inference for optimal treatment regimes; the direct publisher full text was access-restricted.

Develops adaptive smoothing for the mean outcome under an optimal treatment regime and derives an attained asymptotic-variance lower bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class.
Constructs softmax-smoothed inference for optimal-policy values, uses a constant number of nuisance fits, and states efficiency when treatment nonresponse has zero probability.

source_verification_receipts.version: Quantitative Economics 11(1) (2020), pp. 1–39, version of record landing page
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Constructs bias-aware honest nonparametric confidence intervals and derives efficiency and bandwidth results under predeclared function-class restrictions.
Uses the Tsybakov margin condition together with Hölder regression and strong-density classes for plug-in classification.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 112(520) (2017), pp. 1381–1392
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Studies personalized treatment regimes for rilpivirine versus efavirenz using observational HIV data.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair the phase-boundary OEQ: retain δ_n≈n^{-ξ} for every admissible pair, and ask separately whether a root-n union-honest upper interval is attainable when 3s_1α≥d or whether another lower-bound family establishes a binding obstruction there.
Turn the lower theorem into a sharp causal adaptation frontier by proving a connected interval that attains the n^{-ξ} exact-tie length up to constants or logarithms over the same ordered pair.

Add a source-located comparison table against WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, XuGuo2026, ShiLuSong2020, and CaiLow2004 that distinguishes pointwise validity, nominal coverage, and uniform expected-length honesty.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision on this angle: correct the nonbinding-above-boundary phase statement, then retain the explicit sparse-mixture lower theorem as the field-tier focal kernel.
